scenarios.title: selects option by visible text and verifies selection
scenarios.userAction: Navigate to the web form, select the dropdown option whose visible label is 'Three', then confirm the dropdown reflects 'Three' as the current selection.
expectedAssertions: After selectOption({ label: 'Three' }): the dropdown widget value reflects the 'Three' option as selected

locatorTable.notes: Mechanical CSS translation of By.name(x) → [name='x']. Prefer page.getByLabel(...) if reviewer confirms an associated <label> element exists in the DOM (Q1). Upgrade does not affect test correctness, only locator quality score.

toHaveValue() targets the option's value attribute, not its visible text. Assumes value attribute equals visible text 'Three'. If value differs, fallback is: page.locator('select[name="my-select"] option:checked').toHaveText('Three'). See Q2 and pin 1.

hallucinationDefensePins.whyComment: Q2 unresolved: option value attribute assumed to equal visible text 'Three' — confirm via DOM inspection
hallucinationDefensePins.reviewerFallback: Open DevTools on the web-form page, inspect <option> elements inside the my-select dropdown, note the value attribute for 'Three'; if it differs from 'Three', either pass the actual value to toHaveValue() or switch the assertion to page.locator('select[name="my-select"] option:checked').toHaveText('Three')
